seo_home_title: Ragmir: confidential local RAG library for coding agents
seo_home_description: Open-source TypeScript RAG library, CLI, and local MCP server for cited offline retrieval from confidential project files.
seo_home_keywords: Ragmir, confidential local RAG, TypeScript RAG library, coding agents, AI coding agents, cited retrieval, private specifications, monorepo documentation, PDF search, XLSX search, synced Drive files, local automation scripts, persistent Node.js RAG client, Model Context Protocol, MCP
seo_image_alt: Ragmir confidential local RAG with cited retrieval for coding agents
hero_title: Confidential local RAG for your coding agents.
hero_description: Ragmir turns specs, Word files, PDFs, and local exports into cited evidence indexed and retrieved locally. Core works offline by default, never uploads your corpus, and calls no model.
demo_scenario_word_description: Turn a private Word spec into cited code and tests without uploading the file.
demo_scenario_monorepo_description: Query each isolated knowledge base, then assemble one sourced architecture map.
demo_scenario_drive_description: Turn locally synced Drive files into cited implementation context.
demo_scenario_youtube_description: Retrieve cited research locally, draft with your preferred AI, then approve before publishing.
demo_scenario_visa_description: Keep a dated checklist grounded in local documents and human review.
demo_word_prompt: Implement account recovery from these requirements. Cite every rule.
demo_word_search_command: ragmir_search({ query: "account recovery lockout acceptance criteria" })
demo_monorepo_prompt: Explain how authentication works across packages. Cite each owner and contract.
demo_drive_prompt: Implement team invitations from the roadmap, stories, and architecture. Cite every rule.
demo_drive_search_command: ragmir_search({ query: "feature scope dependencies acceptance criteria" })
demo_youtube_prompt: Draft the episode from this cited brief and follow the channel voice guide.
demo_word_out_setup: Codex helper and local MCP ready. .ragmir/ stays Git-ignored.
demo_word_out_rules: Rules: the token expires after 15 minutes, five failed attempts lock the account, and the email must not reveal whether the account exists.
demo_word_out_done: Account recovery code and E2E coverage are grounded in the two cited Word files.
demo_monorepo_out_plan: Three bounded searches, three labeled sources, one architecture map.
demo_monorepo_out_done: Onboarding map grounded in evidence from each isolated base.
demo_drive_out_sync: Google Drive already synchronized this folder. Ragmir reads the local files only.
demo_drive_out_indexed: Indexed locally: 1 PDF, 1 XLSX, 1 DOCX, and research notes | 116 chunks.
demo_drive_out_plan: Implementation order: team model, invitation expiry, privacy-safe email, then cited acceptance tests.
demo_drive_out_done: The feature and tests stay linked to the roadmap, stories, and architecture files.
demo_youtube_out_indexed: Indexed locally: research PDF, notes, and voice guide | 89 chunks.
demo_youtube_script_research: exec: npx rgr research "evidence behind consistent habits and motivation" --no-code --compact --json
demo_youtube_out_done: Draft saved for human review. Nothing was published automatically.
demo_visa_out_indexed: Indexed locally: official guidance, appointments, checklist, and receipts | 67 chunks.
demo_visa_script_research: exec: npx rgr research "documented requirements deadlines missing evidence" --no-code --compact --json
demo_visa_out_done: Dated checklist saved for human review. Verify every action against current official sources.
privacy_title: Keep the corpus and index local. Control what leaves.
privacy_text: Ragmir has no telemetry and never uploads your corpus to a hosted RAG. Local consumers can keep the full workflow offline; a cloud agent receives retrieved passages under that provider's data policy when you choose that workflow.
privacy_docs_text: You point Ragmir at the relevant folder. Private files stay in the repository or the local workspace.
privacy_index_text: The index, reports, and generated integrations stay in the local git-ignored .ragmir/ folder.
privacy_redaction_text: Redaction, size limits, and auditing of unsupported files avoid claiming to index more than what is reliable.
privacy_access_text: Access logs contain metadata only, so usage can be checked without exposing sensitive content.
library_title: Install once. Retrieve through CLI, TypeScript, or MCP.
library_text: Add Ragmir Core to the repository that owns the files, choose the sources, then ingest. Generated state stays under ignored .ragmir/ folders, and the default local-hash path needs no account, API key, or model download.
quickstart_text: Let your coding agent tailor the setup, or choose your package manager and run the commands yourself.
quickstart_prompt_title: Let your coding agent configure Ragmir
quickstart_prompt_text: Paste this prompt into your coding agent. It inspects the repository, asks before making choices, installs the right packages, and verifies cited retrieval.
features_title: Local cited retrieval first. Optional local answers and audio when needed.
features_text: Core retrieves cited project evidence for your coding agent. Optional Chat generates local answers from those passages; optional TTS renders approved text as audio.
features_core_text: Retrieve cited project evidence with adaptive local indexing for agents, scripts, and Node.js workers.
features_core_item_1: Preview redacted chunks and explain retrieval rankings.
features_core_item_2: Reuse one persistent local client in a Node.js worker.
features_core_item_3: Return verifiable file, line, chunk, and PDF page citations.
features_core_item_4: Run offline and keep generated state under ignored .ragmir/.
features_tts_text: Render reviewed, cited project text as local audio for agents and scripts.
features_tts_item_1: Render local text as offline WAV or explicit online MP3.
features_tts_item_2: Work from cited research and reports.
features_tts_item_3: Render WAV offline after model preload.
features_tts_item_4: Keep audio under ignored .ragmir/audio; Edge TTS stays opt-in.
features_chat_text: Turn retrieved project evidence into cited local answers for agents and scripts.
features_chat_item_1: Ground every answer in cited Ragmir passages.
features_chat_item_2: Choose verified Qwen or Gemma profiles.
features_chat_item_3: Use Metal, CUDA, or Vulkan where supported.
features_chat_item_4: Download during setup, then answer offline without a hosted API.
agents_title: An evidence layer for agents, not another memory system.
agents_text: Connect Ragmir to the agent you prefer through CLI or MCP, or keep the complete workflow on the workstation. MCP starts with at most three compact citations by default; research may add three code matches. The agent expands only the evidence it needs. Core never depends on a specific model.
agents_portable_text: Export the active index, required model, embedded read-only runtime, skills, and MCP adapters. Verify it after moving; raw source files and logs stay out.
agents_targets_text: Send bounded cited passages to the agent you already use, keep the handoff local, use optional Chat for an offline answer, or consume retrieval results without any model.
agents_preferred_text: Claude, Codex, Kimi, Hermes, and other compatible clients can consume cited passages through CLI or MCP. A hosted client's data policy applies to those passages.
agents_local_text: OpenCode or another local CLI or MCP consumer can keep retrieved passages on the workstation. No hosted Ragmir service is involved.
agents_offline_text: Optional Ragmir Chat generates cited answers from one verified local GGUF. Qwen and Gemma are included profile choices, not Core requirements.
agents_automation_text: Local scripts, desktop tools, and CI jobs consume typed or JSON retrieval results with or without a generative model.
use_cases_title: Five local workflows built around realistic project files.
use_cases_text: From private specifications and monorepos to synced Drive folders, media research, and administrative dossiers, each flow keeps indexing local, retrieves offline, and returns citations.
use_case_spec_title: Build from a private Word spec.
use_case_spec_description: Turn a private specification into implementation-ready evidence.
use_case_spec_request: Codex receives an account-recovery feature to build.
use_case_spec_evidence: Private Word specifications stay on the developer's machine.
use_case_spec_retrieval: Ragmir retrieves only the matching rules and cites their exact source.
use_case_spec_result: Codex implements and tests the feature against those cited rules.
use_case_monorepo_description: Answer architecture questions across root and package documentation.
use_case_monorepo_question: A developer asks how authentication works across the monorepo.
use_case_monorepo_retrieval: The developer queries each relevant root or package base separately; Ragmir never merges their indexes.
use_case_monorepo_result: The agent returns an onboarding map with owners, dependencies, and sources.
use_case_drive_title: Build from a synced Drive folder.
use_case_drive_description: Reconcile product files before a coding agent implements a feature.
use_case_drive_evidence: Roadmap, spreadsheet, and architecture files share one synced local folder.
use_case_drive_request: A coding agent receives a feature request with missing context.
use_case_drive_retrieval: Ragmir retrieves matching requirements with citations; the agent compares them and identifies conflicts.
use_case_drive_plan: The agent orders the dependencies and acceptance criteria from that evidence.
use_case_drive_result: The feature and tests stay traceable to the product sources.
use_case_youtube_title: Draft a YouTube script backed by citations.
use_case_youtube_description: Ground a draft in research and voice rules before human approval.
use_case_youtube_evidence: Research PDFs, notes, and voice rules stay in one local corpus.
use_case_youtube_request: A local drafting script starts with the next episode topic.
use_case_youtube_retrieval: Ragmir finds the relevant facts, quotes, and voice rules with citations.
use_case_youtube_result: Your AI drafts the episode from that evidence, then a human approves it.
use_case_visa_title: Ground a visa project in local documents.
use_case_visa_description: Keep a dated project checklist grounded in a changing local corpus.
use_case_visa_request: A local project script gathers forms, receipts, and current official guidance.
use_case_visa_retrieval: Ragmir finds matching requirements, deadlines, and missing evidence with citations.
use_case_visa_result: The script updates a dated checklist for human verification before any action.
seo_team_description: Meet the team building Ragmir, an open-source confidential local RAG for coding agents and scripts grounded in cited project documents.
team_member_jb_description: Product developer and founder of JCode Labs. He builds Ragmir to give coding agents and local scripts cited evidence from specifications and documentation without uploading the entire corpus to a hosted RAG.
team_mission_description_1: Ragmir gives developers and teams a traceable evidence layer for private project knowledge. The corpus and index stay on each workstation, and you choose whether cited passages go to a local consumer or a preferred cloud agent.
team_mission_description_2: Use the same CLI, TypeScript API, or MCP interface in solo and team workflows, with no hosted Ragmir account or document store.
faq_text: What Ragmir keeps local, how teams align their sources, which optional setup commands use the network, and how to connect agents.
faq_what_answer: Ragmir is an open-source confidential local RAG for coding agents and scripts on Node.js: a library, CLI, and local MCP server that turns the files you choose into cited, queryable context. It complements agent memory and native code search for specifications, runbooks, contracts, PDFs, and other project documents. The default path works offline with no model download.
faq_private_question: Does Ragmir send my confidential code or documents?
faq_private_answer: Ragmir indexes only the local folders you choose and keeps generated state in the git-ignored .ragmir/ folder. Core retrieval has no telemetry or document upload. Explicit model setup downloads public model weights, never your documents; cloud agents can receive cited passages when you choose to use them.
faq_agents_question: Which AI agents and tools does Ragmir work with?
faq_agents_answer: Ragmir Core is model-agnostic. Integrations cover Claude Code, Codex, Kimi, OpenCode, and Cline. Other MCP clients, local scripts, desktop tools, and CI jobs use the same cited results.
faq_portable_question: Can I move a Ragmir knowledge base to another agent or server?
faq_portable_answer: Yes. rgr portable export creates one frozen folder with the active index, any required local embedding model, an embedded read-only runtime, two skills, MCP adapters, and a SHA-256 inventory. Move it to a matching Node.js 22 platform, verify it without a package-manager install or registry access, then connect Claude, Codex, Kimi, OpenCode, Cline, or a compatible MCP or CLI host. Later, --replace verifies the next export before switching the stable path and preserves the prior bundle as a timestamped backup. Raw source files and logs are excluded, but indexed passages remain sensitive; the host keeps action authority and network security.
faq_team_question: How does a team share the same knowledge base?
faq_team_answer: Teams merge reviewed changes into a declared Git upstream, then run rgr team sync. Ragmir fast-forwards only a clean, non-divergent branch and refreshes each developer's private local index. --no-pull keeps branch updates manual; advanced metadata-only comparisons remain available for non-Git sources or exact drift diagnostics.
faq_offline_question: Does Ragmir require an API key, a model download, or an internet connection?
faq_offline_answer: No for the default path. Local-hash retrieval runs offline with no model or API key. Team sync fetches the configured Git upstream only when invoked; --no-fetch keeps that run offline. For a fully offline workflow, use a local consumer or optional Ragmir Chat, which downloads and verifies one selected GGUF profile once, then generates locally. No Ollama, Python, or hosted LLM API is required.
faq_observability_question: Can I see where ingestion time and memory are spent?
faq_observability_answer: Yes. Optional ingestion metrics attribute discovery, hashing, parsing, redaction, chunking, embedding, local index writes, maintenance, throughput, queues, cache state, and peak RSS. The bounded local diagnostic contains no project root, source path, source text, or query, and its timers stay off unless requested or subscribed.
faq_compare_question: How does Ragmir differ from IDE code indexing or hosted RAG?
faq_compare_answer: Native code search handles structured code well. Ragmir targets project documents that need citations, keeps its index local, and exposes bounded passages through non-destructive MCP tools. Small output budgets keep exact citations and return typed summaries with omission counts.
faq_role_question: Does Ragmir write the answers for me?
faq_role_answer: Ragmir Core returns cited passages from your local files; the agent or application you choose decides what to do with them. Optional rgr chat adds cited answer generation from a verified local profile when the response must also stay on the workstation.
faq_formats_question: Which file formats can Ragmir index?
faq_formats_answer: Source code, text, Markdown, PDF, Office and OpenDocument, EPUB, HTML, CSV, JSON, and YAML, plus the custom text extensions you enable. Unsupported files are flagged explicitly. Scanned PDFs can use optional local OCR with bounded page batches and a private resumable cache.
faq_server_question: Does Ragmir run as a web server or use a fixed port?
faq_server_answer: No. Use the local stdio MCP server for coding agents or embed the TypeScript client in a stateful Node.js process. Ragmir opens no HTTP port; a network-facing application owns authentication, authorization, rate limits, and transport security.
closing_title: Add confidential local RAG to your agent. Keep your project files local.
closing_text: Connect cited passages to your preferred agent, or prepare a local Chat profile and keep retrieval and answer generation offline. No hosted Ragmir account is required.
footer_text: Ragmir is JCode Labs' confidential local RAG layer for coding agents and scripts that need offline, cited retrieval from private project documents.
strengths_title: Built for the documents your code search does not see.
strengths_text: Ragmir turns private, scattered project documents into a local evidence layer that coding agents and scripts can query offline through MCP, CLI, or TypeScript.
strengths_best_item_1: Search PDFs, DOCX, XLSX, specifications, contracts, and runbooks alongside the repository they explain.
strengths_best_item_2: Start with at most three compact citations, then expand only the selected evidence instead of pasting a large or fragmented corpus into one prompt.
strengths_best_item_3: Keep the index local and pair Ragmir with a local consumer when no cited passage may leave the workstation.
strengths_best_item_4_title: Evidence agents and scripts can reuse
strengths_best_item_4: Give coding agents and local scripts traceable passages with file, line, chunk, and PDF page references.
strengths_limits_title: Add cited project knowledge without replacing your tools.
strengths_limits_text: Ragmir works alongside repository search, your preferred agent, and the file-sharing workflow your team already uses.
strengths_limits_item_1: Keep native repository search for code and use Ragmir for the documents around it.
strengths_limits_item_2: Start with a few sources, then grow the same local workflow as the corpus expands.
strengths_limits_item_3: Synchronize shared source folders with existing team tools and build a local index per developer.
